data.body:
PASS 1 (E4 — assumption/premise verification) for Cluster 1: CLEAN, zero findings. Every codebase assertion was Read-verified against live source:
- `_store/canonical.py` current signature `canonical_str(event)` + `ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")` — confirmed (lines 39-47).
- Six positional importers (`event_append`, `_commands/{txn,delete,compact}`, `rebar_reconciler/{inbound_translate,batch_dispatch}`) all call positionally → byte-identical under additive kw-only params — confirmed via grep.
- `signing.py:_canonical_payload` uses `ensure_ascii=False` (line 198) → seam default — confirmed.
- `llm/workflow/schema.py:canonical_json`/`content_hash` use `ensure_ascii=False` (line 396) and are re-exported in `llm/workflow/__init__.py __all__` (lines 85-86) — confirmed.
- `conflict_resolver.py:_hash_value` (line 217-219) omits `ensure_ascii` (stdlib default True) and passes `default=str` → `ascii_only=True, default=str` claim is load-bearing and CORRECT.
- `mutation.py:serialize_manifest` uses `ensure_ascii=True` (line 106) → `ascii_only=True` preserve claim CORRECT.
No unverifiable or contradicted premises.
